story.so_that: The codebase is maintainable, testable, and can support multiple agent types in the future
story.i_want: Clear separation of concerns between the DevinWrapper (thin CLI adapter) and the Orchestrator (instrumentation and state management)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: DevinWrapper accepts prompt, agent_config, optional timeout, and optional session_id
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a prompt and agent configuration When DevinWrapper.execute_prompt is called Then it returns (output, session_id) tuple

acceptance_criteria.criterion: DevinWrapper does not manage session files, logging, or verification scripts
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given DevinWrapper execution When the wrapper completes Then no session files or logs are written by the wrapper

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Orchestrator loads step definition data and enriches prompts before calling wrapper
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a step_id When orchestrator.invoke_step is called Then the orchestrator loads step data and enriches the prompt

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Orchestrator manages session lifecycle (session_id.txt, feedback.txt, stderr.txt)
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given wrapper execution When orchestrator receives output Then orchestrator writes session state files

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Orchestrator invokes verification scripts after step execution
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given step execution completion When orchestrator processes results Then orchestrator invokes the step's verification script

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Generic AgentWrapper interface enables future agent implementations
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a new agent type When implementing AgentWrapper interface Then the new agent can be used without modifying orchestrator

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Timeout enforcement happens at orchestrator level, not wrapper level
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a timeout parameter When orchestrator calls wrapper Then orchestrator enforces the timeout around the wrapper call

edge_cases.gherkin: Given an empty prompt When wrapper is called Then wrapper passes to CLI as-is
Given invalid agent configuration When wrapper is called Then wrapper fails gracefully with error output
Given non-existent session_id When wrapper attempts resumption Then wrapper fails and creates new session
Given timeout exceeded When wrapper executes Then orchestrator kills wrapper process and captures partial output

edge_cases.criterion: Verification script missing or not executable
edge_cases.gherkin: Given missing verification script When orchestrator invokes verification Then orchestrator logs error and continues

orchestrator: orchestrator/devin_wrapper.py - Refactor to thin CLI adapter
orchestrator/run_saga.py - Update to use new orchestrator interface

steps.description: Create AgentWrapper abstract base class with execute_prompt() interface
steps.details: Define abstract base class that DevinWrapper and future agents will implement. Include timeout, session_id, and error handling contracts.

steps.description: Refactor DevinWrapper to implement AgentWrapper interface
steps.details: Remove session file management, verification script invocation, and enrichment logic. Keep only CLI wrapping and timeout handling.

steps.description: Create Orchestrator class with invoke_step() method
steps.details: New class that handles step loading, enrichment, wrapper invocation, state management, and verification.
Call substitute_variables() before passing prompt to wrapper. Handle enrichment failures.

steps.description: Move session file management to Orchestrator
steps.details: Orchestrator writes session_id.txt, feedback.txt, stderr.txt after wrapper execution.

steps.description: Move verification script invocation to Orchestrator
steps.details: Orchestrator invokes verification script after wrapper execution and captures exit code.

steps.description: Move timeout enforcement to Orchestrator level
steps.details: Orchestrator enforces timeout around entire step lifecycle using subprocess.run(timeout=...).
Replace direct wrapper calls with orchestrator.invoke_step(). Update routing logic to use returned exit code.
Create Orchestrator instance and pass to SagaExecutor for step execution.

steps.description: Write unit tests for AgentWrapper interface
steps.details: Test that DevinWrapper correctly implements interface. Test mock agent implementations.

steps.description: Write unit tests for DevinWrapper refactoring
steps.details: Verify wrapper is stateless. Verify no files are written by wrapper. Test error handling.
Test step loading, enrichment, wrapper invocation, state management, verification invocation.

steps.description: Write integration tests for step execution flow
steps.details: Test full flow: load step -> enrich -> execute -> manage state -> verify. Test with real step definitions.

steps.description: Write integration tests for session resumption
steps.details: Test that session ID is captured and can be used to resume execution.
Update tests to work with new orchestrator interface. Verify backward compatibility.

unit_tests.description: AgentWrapper interface contract - test that DevinWrapper implements all required methods
DevinWrapper - test execute_prompt() returns (output, session_id) tuple
DevinWrapper - verify no session files are written by wrapper
DevinWrapper - test error handling for missing CLI
DevinWrapper - test timeout parameter is passed to subprocess
Orchestrator - test prompt enrichment with EnrichmentDictionary
Orchestrator - test session state file writing
Orchestrator - test timeout enforcement around wrapper call
Orchestrator - test error handling for missing step definition
Orchestrator - test error handling for enrichment failure

integration_tests.description: Full step execution flow - load step, enrich prompt, execute, manage state, verify
Session resumption - capture session ID and resume execution
SagaExecutor integration - verify SagaExecutor calls Orchestrator.invoke_step()
Timeout enforcement - verify orchestrator kills wrapper on timeout
Verification failure handling - verify verification exit code determines routing
Enrichment failure handling - verify orchestrator fails step on enrichment error

e2e_tests.description: Execute complete saga with multiple steps using new orchestrator
Execute saga with step retry (traversal limit) using new orchestrator
Execute saga with sub-saga using new orchestrator

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Breaking change to existing saga execution - SagaExecutor needs to be updated to use new Orchestrator interface
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Provide clear migration path. Update SagaExecutor incrementally. Maintain backward compatibility during transition.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Session file management moved from wrapper to orchestrator - existing code that reads session files may break
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Session file location and format remain unchanged. Only responsibility moves, not interface.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Timeout enforcement moved from wrapper to orchestrator - timing behavior may change
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Orchestrator enforces timeout around entire step lifecycle (enrichment + execution + verification). Test timeout behavior thoroughly.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Enrichment integration may fail if EnrichmentDictionary is not properly initialized
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Orchestrator should validate enrichment dictionary before use. Fail step with clear error if enrichment fails.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Verification script invocation moved from wrapper to orchestrator - verification behavior may change
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Verification script location and invocation method remain unchanged. Only responsibility moves, not interface.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Generic AgentWrapper interface may not be flexible enough for future agent types
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Design interface to be minimal and extensible. Use composition for agent-specific features. Plan for interface evolution.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Concurrent step execution may cause session file conflicts
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Use unique session IDs per invocation. ADR-009 will define state persistence structure for isolation.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Tests may fail due to refactoring - existing test suite needs updates
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Update tests incrementally. Maintain test coverage throughout refactoring. Use mocking for isolation.
